run.output:
Query returned 1 row
Execution time: 126 ms

validation.comparison_result: Results do not match. Expected revenue: 1230113636.0101, Got: 0.0000. CRITICAL: All discount values in GenDB binary storage (lineitem_l_discount.col) are 0x00000000. This is a data generation bug—TPC-H text data contains valid discount values (0.05-0.10 range) but binary conversion failed.

Q6.notes: CRITICAL DATA BUG: GenDB discount column is all zeros. Text source (lineitem.tbl) has valid values, but binary conversion corrupted data. Q6 code logic is correct (filters properly), but no rows pass filter because all discounts are 0 instead of 0.05-0.10 range. Execution time 126ms is ~4x slower than GenDB baseline (29.3ms) due to: (1) not using zone maps on shipdate filter, (2) sequential filtering without SIMD, (3) no parallelization despite 64 available cores.

Q6.bottleneck: Data integrity: discount column all zeros. Additionally: I/O-bound (mmap sequential scan), missing parallelization, missing zone map filtering.
Q6.root_cause: PRIMARY: GenDB storage layer corrupted discount column during binary conversion (lineitem_l_discount.col = all 0x00000000). Text source TPC-H data has valid 0.05-0.10 values, but binary conversion failed. SECONDARY: Code structure is correct but runs 4x slower than baseline due to single-threaded execution and missing optimizations.

analysis.overall: Q6 iter_2 fails correctness due to GenDB data bug (discount column is all zeros), not due to code logic. The code correctly reads binary columns and applies proper filters, but no rows match because ALL discounts are 0. This is a data generation bug in GenDB's binary conversion, not a query code issue. Once data is fixed, performance optimizations should target: (1) parallel scan with 64 threads, (2) zone map utilization for shipdate range, (3) SIMD-accelerated filtering, (4) branch-free predicate evaluation.

critical_fixes.issue: CRITICAL SEMANTIC/DATA: All discount values in GenDB binary storage are 0x00000000. Text source (lineitem.tbl) has valid discount values (0.05-0.10), but binary conversion corrupted the column. Query returns 0.0000 instead of 1230113636.0101 because no rows pass discount filter.
critical_fixes.fix: Regenerate GenDB storage: ensure lineitem_l_discount.col is populated from lineitem.tbl column 7 (discount) with proper int32_t encoding (value × 100, e.g., 0.06 → 6). Verify encoding matches storage_design.json specification.
critical_fixes.description: The issue is in GenDB's data loading, not in Q6 code. The q6.cpp correctly: (1) loads binary columns via mmap, (2) applies proper filter predicates (discount BETWEEN 5 AND 7, where 5=0.05 and 7=0.07), (3) computes revenue with correct scaling (price × discount / 10000). However, lineitem_l_discount.col contains all zeros instead of values in 0-10 range (representing 0.00-0.10). This is a GenDB bug. To fix: Regenerate the binary column file from the text data, ensuring proper type conversion and encoding. Current state: hexdump shows 60M rows of 0x00000000.

performance_optimizations.technique: Morsel-Driven Parallel Scan with Thread-Local Aggregation
performance_optimizations.description: After data fix: Current code is single-threaded. With 64 cores available, implement: (1) Partition 60M lineitem rows into 64 morsels (~1M rows per thread). (2) Each thread: scan filter predicates in tight loop, accumulate SUM to thread-local double. (3) Use alignment to 64B cache-line boundary on LocalSum struct to prevent false sharing (already present in current code structure). (4) Final sequential reduction merges thread-local results. Expected: 40-50x speedup on 64 cores with near-linear scaling for this CPU-bound scan pattern.
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: 40-50x (126ms → 2.5-3ms). Linear scaling up to 64 cores for scan+filter+aggregate pattern.

performance_optimizations.technique: Zone Map Utilization for Range Predicates
performance_optimizations.description: After data fix + parallelization: Storage design includes zone_map index on l_shipdate. Implement: (1) Load zone_map metadata (min/max values per block). (2) Before scanning each block, check if block's shipdate range [min, max] overlaps with filter range [8766, 9131). (3) Skip entire 256K-row blocks if disjoint. With 60M rows in ~250 blocks and moderate date selectivity (15.7%), expected: 10-15% block skip rate = 10-15% I/O savings. After parallelization, this reduces thread contention on disk I/O.
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: 1.1-1.2x (after parallelization). 10-15% reduction in scanned rows → 10-15% less cache pressure and I/O.

performance_optimizations.description: After parallelization + zone map: Optimize inner loop filter chain. Current code: sequential if-statements cause branch mispredictions on random data. Implement: (1) Reorder filters by selectivity (discount 11% most selective) evaluated first. (2) Use branch-free predicates: compute boolean mask for all conditions in parallel without branches. (3) Leverage SIMD: use AVX2 to process 8×int32_t quantities in single instruction, compare against threshold 24. (4) For discount filter: load 8×int32_t discounts, compare all 8 against [5,7] range in single SIMD operation. (5) Combine masks with bitwise AND, skip (or accumulate) based on final mask. Expected: eliminates ~3-4 branch mispredictions per row (on random data, 30-40% miss rate with sequential if-statements) = 1.3-1.5x from reduced branch penalty + SIMD overhead amortization.
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: 1.3-1.5x. Branch-free filtering eliminates mispredictions; SIMD adds 1.3x on 64-bit quantity comparisons (8 values per register).

summary: Q6 iteration 2 FAILS due to a critical data bug in GenDB: the discount column (lineitem_l_discount.col) contains all zeros instead of valid discount values. This is not a code bug—the Q6 implementation is structurally correct and uses proper binary columnar access. However, because all discounts are 0 and the filter requires BETWEEN 0.05 AND 0.07 (encoded as 5-7), zero rows match. Expected result: 1230113636.0101; actual: 0.0000. The code logic is sound: it loads binary columns, applies correct filters, and computes revenue with proper scaling (price × discount / 10000). Once GenDB's data generation is fixed, performance optimizations should target: (1) parallelization across 64 cores (~40-50x speedup), (2) zone map utilization for shipdate range skip (~1.1x), (3) SIMD-accelerated branch-free filtering (~1.3x). Current single-threaded execution is also 4x slower than baseline, indicating sequential scan inefficiency. Iteration 2 regressed from iteration 1 in one sense (now has correct data format but data is still wrong), but the architecture is cleaner. Recommend: fix GenDB data generation first, then implement parallelization + zone maps + SIMD filtering.
